metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_2580
metadata.comment:
Rationale: TARP γ-4 modulates the decay of synaptic AMPAR currents.

Fig. 7: Overexpression of stargazin (stg, TARP γ-2) or TARP γ-4 in cultured cerebellar granule cells from stg mutant (stg/stg) mice restored AMPAR-mediated synaptic responses. The amplitude and frequency of mEPSCs in stg/stg cells transfected with TARP γ-4 were ~50% of WT. The decay of mEPSCs and weighted decay constants were slower in TARP γ-4 expressing neurons compared to TARP γ-2 or WT cells.

The differential slowing of the decay of mEPSCs by TARP γ-4 in neurons was similar to its effect on slowing of deactivation decays for GluR1 channels in heterologous cells, "demonstrating that the isoform-specific modulation of gating kinetics characterized here is relevant to synaptic transmission in neurons.". Experimental description: From "EXPERIMENTAL PROCEDURES"

Studies on Recombinant Channels: tsA201 cells were maintained and transfected as previously described (Robert and Howe, 2003). All GluR plasmids used were flip splice variants. Both Q and R variants of GluR2 were used as indicated. Individual GluR and TARP clones were cotransfected at ratios of 1:2 to 1:5. In the GluR1-GluR2 coexpression experiments, the flip version of GluR2- R was employed, and equal amounts of the two GluR plasmids were transfected....

Synaptic Currents in Transfected Cerebellar Granule Cells: Stargazer mice were obtained from Jackson Laboratory...Heterozygous male and female mice were mated to obtain wild-type and homozygous stargazer mice. Cerebellar granule cell cultures were prepared from postnatal day 7 (P7) wild-type and homozygous stargazer mice as described (Losi et al., 2003).
